text: Coming from the argument in Romans 7:7–25, Romans 8:3–4 says that the law cannot solve the problem of sin. The law’s failure is a big part of the larger problem of sin that needed a fix. I see the point most clearly when the opening clause of Romans 8:3 (τὸ ... ἀδύνατον) is read as a case of respect with extra clauses, not as a broken sentence that many think. I notice that the opening clause points out that sin has two problems. Those two problems are the key points that the writer of Romans 8:3–4 refers to. Romans 8:3–4 shows how Paul deals with a three part problem. In Romans 8:3, Paul says that the work of God’s Son solves the problem of sin and solves the problem of weak flesh. Paul says that the judgment of sin in the flesh of Christ ends the problem of sin and ends the problem of weak flesh. The purpose is to solve the problem of the law’s inability by having Christ fulfill the law’s requirement (Rm 8:4). Romeine 8:3–4 and God’s solution to the three‑part problem of sin, the law’s inability and the flesh’s weakness. In the light of Romeine 7:7–25, Romeine 8:3–4 shows that the law’s inability was a major part of the larger problem of sin needed solving. I see that the law’s inability and the flesh’s weakness together keep people trapped in sin, and that Christ’s work opens a way out. Ek kry insig wanneer ek die sin wat Romeine 8:3 begin, die Griekse frase τὸ ... ἀδύνατον, as ‘n akkusatief van opsig herken. Ek sien die sin nie as ‘n anakoluthon nie, soos sommige mense dink. Paul wys twee probleme wat met sonde kom. Die eerste probleem is die wet wat kan nie help nie. Die tweede probleem is die vlees wat is swak. Paul gebruik hierdie twee probleme as die belangrikste punte. Paul bou die uitsprake in Romeine 8:3–4 op hierdie twee probleme. In Romeine 8:3 wys Paulus dat God, deur sy Seun te stuur, die probleme van die sonde en die vlees aangespreek het deurdat die sonde in die vlees (van Christus) veroordeel is. Dit het die doel-gevolg gehad dat die meegaande probleem van die onvermoë van die wet ook opgelos is, aangesien Christus die vereiste van die wet vervul het (Rom 8:4).
explanation: The text uses nuanced theological analysis and includes Afrikaans in addition to English, which is characteristic of human scholarly writing; while AI can imitate, this level of bilingual, domain-specific reasoning is more likely human-authored.
